findings.evidence: Lines 64–65: `unipotent_radical_trivial : True`; lines 69–70: `radical_trivial : True`. Any value of type `True` is `trivial`, so these add no constraint.
findings.fix: Replace the `True` fields with the actual mathematical conditions (or mark them `sorry`/`axiom` with a docstring that honestly states 'placeholder pending full development'), and update the class and theorem docstrings to match what is actually encoded.
findings.issue: `ReductiveGroup` has `unipotent_radical_trivial : True` — a field that is always trivially inhabited — so the class imposes no condition on the unipotent radical at all. The docstring claiming 'unipotent radical … is trivial' overclaims: every `AffineAlgGroup` automatically satisfies this class. Same pattern in `SemisimpleGroup` (line 68, `radical_trivial : True`). `ReductiveGroup.unipotentRadical_trivial'` (line 72) is correspondingly a vacuous theorem whose docstring also overclaims.

findings.evidence: Line 49: `def IsUnipotent (g : GroupLike k A)`. `AlgPoints` is defined on line 42 as `A →ₐ[k] k`; these are distinct types.
findings.fix: Change the docstring to 'An element `g : GroupLike k A` of the coordinate ring is unipotent if `(g - 1)` is nilpotent in `A`.'
findings.issue: `IsUnipotent` docstring says 'g ∈ G(k)' but `g : GroupLike k A` is a group-like element of the coordinate ring `A`, not a `k`-algebra homomorphism `A → k`. `G(k)` in the Hopf-algebra picture is `AlgPoints k A = A →ₐ[k] k`.

findings.evidence: Mathlib `IsGeometricallyReduced` (GeometricallyReduced.lean:54): requires `IsReduced (AlgebraicClosure p.ResidueField ⊗[R] A)` — no regularity or Jacobian condition.
findings.fix: Remove '(smooth)' from both docstrings, or add a note that smoothness follows in characteristic 0 by Cartier's theorem but is not part of the definition.
findings.issue: Module docstring (line 16) and `AffineAlgGroup` docstring (line 34) both write 'geometrically reduced (smooth)', equating `IsGeometricallyReduced` with smoothness. Geometrically reduced means reduced after base change to an algebraic closure; smooth is strictly stronger.

summary: Three documentation inaccuracies: the `ReductiveGroup` and `SemisimpleGroup` classes contain vacuous `True` fields but their docstrings claim they enforce real mathematical conditions; the `IsUnipotent` docstring misidentifies the type of its argument; and both the module header and `AffineAlgGroup` docstring conflate geometrically reduced with smooth.
